rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re Contempt
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Michael B. McNaughton. On June 3, 2024, this court issued, after noticed ex parte hearing, Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee ("Order"). Paragraph Four of the order states: "Respondent Mark Borsuk is ordered to immediately deliver to professional private fiduciary David A. Bradlow all assets and records of the Trust Created Under the Last Will and Testament of Etta Bacharach." On March 20, 2026, following hearing on Temporary Trustee David A. Bradlow's Motion to Compel Compliance and for Order to Show Cause Why Respondent Mark Borsuk Should Not Be Held in Contempt, the court concurrently issued two orders: Order After Hearing On Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Order To Show Cause Re Contempt To Issue; and Order To Show Cause Re Contempt. In the Order to Show Cause, Mark Borsuk was ordered to show cause why he should not be held in contempt for failing to comply with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee, and fined $1,000 and ordered to pay the reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred by temporary trustee David A. Bradlow in connection with the contempt proceeding, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sec. 1218(a). Mr. Borsuk timely filed a response to the Order to Show Cause. Temporary Trustee David A. Bradlow filed a reply to the response, with supporting declaration of counsel. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1209(a)(5), disobedience of any lawful order of the court is a contempt of the authority of the court. The court finds as follows: (1) the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee was a valid order; (2) Mark Borsuk had actual knowledge of the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee; (3) Mark Borsuk was able to comply with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee; (4) Mark Borsuk willfully failed to comply with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee. The court finds Mark Borsuk to be GUILTY OF CONTEMPT, for willfully and knowingly failing to comply with the June 3, 2024 Ex Parte Order Suspending Powers of Mark Borsuk as Trustee and for Order Appointing Temporary Trustee. Pursuant to the court's powers and discretion under Code of Civil Procedure section 1218, the court sanctions Mark Borsuk in the amount of $250, payable to the Clerk of the Court within ten days of entry of this order. Further, pursuant to its discretion under Code of Civil Procedure section 1218(a), the court awards $4,000 in reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred in connection with the contempt proceeding to Temporary Trustee David A. Bradlow. Mark Borsuk shall pay to Temporary Trustee David A. Bradlow the amount of $4,000 within ten days of entry of this order. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
